revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安住院]]
{{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Zenkoji Anjuin | image = File:Anjuuin 04.JPG | caption = Main Hall (Prefectural Important Cultural Property) | location = 3-1-29 Kokutomi, Naka-ku, {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|qid=Q200078|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.663219|N|133.950409|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain = Mikkai-san (瓶井山) | temple name = Anjuin | school = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Kogi Shingon|1=Shingon Buddhism}} | sect = [[Zentsuji school of Shingon Buddhism|Zentsuji sect]] | principal image = [[Thousand-Armed Kannon]] (Secret Buddha) | founded = Said to be established during the [[Tenpyō-Shōhō]] era (749–757) | founder = Said to be [[Hōon Daishi]] | formal name = Mikkaisan Zenkoji Anjuin (瓶井山禪光寺安住院) | pilgrimage = Setouchi 33 Kannon, No. 12; Okayama 88 Temples, No. 5 | cultural property = Nio Gate, Tahōtō Pagoda, Main Hall, Standing Statue of Kannon (Prefectural Important Cultural Properties), Standing Statue of Bishamonten, Donors List for Nio Hall Construction (Municipal Important Cultural Properties) | website = [http://www.anjuin.com Anjuin Official Site] | prefecture= {{ill|Naka-ku|qid=Q852116|1=Naka-ku, Okayama}} }} '''Anjuin''' (安住院) is a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|qid=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temples in Japan|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} located in {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|qid=Q200078|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, Japan. Its mountain name is '''Mikkaisan''' (瓶井山), and it is formally known as '''Mikkaisan Zenkoji Anjuin'''. The temple belongs to the [[Zentsuji sect]] of {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}}. Its principal image is the '''Thousand-Armed Kannon''' (*Senju Sengan Kannon*), which is enshrined as a secret Buddha. The temple is the '''12th temple in the Setouchi 33 Kannon Pilgrimage''' and the '''5th temple in the Okayama 88 Temples Pilgrimage''' (Daishi Hall). == Overview == Anjuin is located on the northwest foothills of [[Sōzan]] (操山). The old name of Sōzan is '''Mikkaisan''', derived from a spring called '''Mikkai''' (*Mikai no Izumi*) found in the temple's grounds. This is the origin of the temple’s mountain name. According to temple tradition, '''Hōon Daishi''' founded Zenkoji during the '''Tenpyō-Shōhō era''' (749–757). It was considered one of the '''Bizen 48 Temples'''. Anjuin serves as the '''main hall of Zenkoji'''. Hōon Daishi is said to have discovered a luminous sacred tree on Hirai Beach (*currently Hirai, Naka-ku, Okayama City*), from which he carved a '''Thousand-Armed Kannon''' as the temple’s principal image. While carving, he recited: "修禅光明滅無明安住浄菩提心" (*Shuzen Komyo Metsu Mumyo Anju Jōbodai Shin*), which became the inspiration for the temple’s name. In '''907''', during the '''Heian period''', '''Shōbō''', the founder of [[:en:Daigoji|Daigoji]], conducted the '''first major restoration'''. In '''1395''', during the '''Muromachi period''', '''Zōun''' led another restoration. However, in '''1478''', all buildings except the '''Nio Gate''' were lost to fire. In '''1601''', during the '''early Edo period''', '''Kobayakawa Hideaki''', the first lord of the '''Okayama Domain''', reconstructed the '''Main Hall''' and other buildings. During the Edo period, the temple received patronage from the '''Ikeda clan''', the feudal lords of Okayama. Anjuin was the '''main temple''' (*honbō*) of Zenkoji and oversaw multiple '''sub-temples''' (*tatchū*), including Fumon-in, Chūzō-in, Gyōō-in, Renge-in, Rishō-in, Hōmyō-in, Kanan-in, Kanki-in, Mantoku-in, Denkyō-in, Kinzō-in, and Myōju-in. Today, only '''Anjuin and Fumon-in''' remain, with Chūzō-in existing only in name. Anjuin is the '''family temple''' (*bodaiji*) of '''Uchida Hyakken''', and his grave, along with those of his ancestors, is located in the temple cemetery. == Cultural Properties == '''According to the Okayama City Cultural Property Register''' ([https://www.city.okayama.jp/shisei/0000005177.html source]): === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties (Tangible Cultural Properties):''' * '''Nio Gate''' (*Niōmon*) – Built in '''1456''' during the mid-Muromachi period. Painted entirely in red, it is known as the "'''Red Gate of Mikkai'''." It is the '''oldest dated two-story gate in Okayama Prefecture'''. * '''Tahōtō Pagoda''' – Also called the '''Sonsyo Hōtō'''. Built during the '''Genroku era''' (1688–1704) by '''Ikeda Tsunamasa''', the Okayama Domain lord, as a '''borrowed landscape''' (*shakkei*) for '''Korakuen Garden'''. Stands at '''19.97m''' in height. * '''Main Hall''' (*Hondō*) – Originally built in '''1601''' by '''Kobayakawa Hideaki''', later relocated in '''1800'''. Designated in '''2018'''. * '''Standing Statue of Kannon''' (*Shō Kannon*) – Carved in '''late 8th to early 9th century''', using the '''Nata-bori''' (*adze carving*) technique. === Okayama City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Standing Statue of Bishamonten''' (*Heian period, 11th century*). * '''Donors List for the Construction of the Nio Hall''' (*1456*). === Formerly Owned Cultural Property === * '''Illustrated Scroll of Buddhist Hells''' (*Jigoku Sōshi*) – *National Treasure* housed in the '''Tokyo National Museum'''. == Pilgrimage Route == ; [[Setouchi 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] : 11 [[Myo-oji (Okayama)|Myo-oji]] → '''12 Anjuin''' → 13 [[Raikyuji]] == External Links == * [http://www.anjuin.com Zenkoji Anjuin Official Website] {{translated page|ja|安住院|version=102419036|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ceb:カナメモチ]] [[lld:Mont Misao]]
